calendar.matters:
Petition Of Gloria A. Tillmon To Determine The Amount Of Reasonable Attorneys' Fees To Be Awarded On Order On Petition To Compel Filing Of Inventory And Appraisal.
Off Calendar Oct-22-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Dorazio appeared by telephone for Gloria Tillmon (Petitioner).

Mr. Dorazio requested an order releasing the amount of attorney's fees from the bond. The Court explained the process Mr. Dorazio would need to take in order to accomplish his request. Mr. Dorazio raised the issue of Mr. Irwin and his Attorney stating that bond was insufficient, Mr. Irwin and his Attorney failing to file an ex parte request for increase in bond, and requested an order for the Personal Representative to appear and show cause as to what he is or is not doing and to explain what is going on with the Estate. Mr. Dorazio also raised the issue regarding the Personal Representative's failure to carry out his duties and the $355.00 fee he paid for filing of the Petition for Attorney's Fees. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Petition For Withdrawal Of Attorney Of Record
Off Calendar Sep-22-2010 Granted Per Written Order

Petition Of Gloria A. Tillmon To Determine The Amount Of Reasonable Attorneys' Fees To Be Awarded On Order On Petition To Compel Filing Of Inventory And Appraisal.
Off Calendar Sep-22-2010 Continued To Oct-20-10 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mark Shustoff for Stephen Irwin. Michael Dorazio appeared by telephone for Gloria Tillman (Beneficiary).

The Court granted the Motion for Withdrawal of Attorney of Record filed by Mr. Shustoff (subject to review of the order). The Court stated that there was the issue of verification of the petition and proof of service on the bonding company. The matter was continued to October 20, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court stated that notice of the petition needs to be sent to Mr. Irwin and to the bonding company.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Petition For Withdrawal Of Attorney Of Record
Off Calendar Aug-18-2010 Continued To Sep-22-10 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition Of Gloria Ann Tillmon To Compel Filing Of Inventory And Appraisal
Off Calendar Jul-21-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mark Shustoff for Stephen Irwin (Administrator). Michael Dorazio appeared by Court Call for Gloria Ann Tillman (Mother of the Decedent).

The Court stated that the Inventory and Appraisal was filed and that Mr. Dorazio requested the matter stay on calendar. Mr. Dorazio stated under Local Rule 7501 that there has to be a statement of a sufficient bond, if not, then the personal rep or Counsel is required to file an ex-parte to increase the bond which has not been done. Mr. Shustoff stated that he has lost contact with his client since last November, that he has written numerous letters/left voicemails, that his letters were returned unable to forward, and that his plan is to file a petition to withdraw as Counsel. Mr. Dorazio stated pursuant to Probate Code 8804c that Mrs. Tillman can recover her attorney fees/costs. Mr. Shustoff stated that he tried to contact Mr. Dorazio to suggest that his client waive the bond but he never heard from him until this petition was filed. Mr. Dorazio disagreed stating that he and Mr. Shustoff had plenty of contact, that his client was getting frustrated, that Mr. Irwin didn't perform his duties, that the final distribution is overdue, and that it is a direct result of Mr. Irwin not filing the Inventory and Appraisal. The Court inquired whether there was injury to the Estate. Mr. Dorazio stated that they aren't claiming injury to the Estate but to an interested party: Decedent's Mother. The Court inquired whether it had anything before it regarding fees. Mr. Dorazio stated no, that he was under the impression that he would get an order to that effect and then submit his request. Mr. Shustoff stated that Mr. Irwin was given the checks and was directed to open an Estate account; that's all he knows. The Court took the matter under submission and confirmed that the file didn't reflect that the bond was exonerated.
Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

Petition Of Gloria Ann Tillmon To Compel Filing Of Inventory And Appraisal
Off Calendar Jun-09-2010 Continued To Jun-30-10 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jun-17-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mark Shustoff for Stephen Irwin (Petitioner). Michael Dorazio for Gloria Tillman (Mother of the Deceased) by Court Call.

Counsel stipulated on the record to the Commissioner sitting as a Judge Pro Tem.

Mr. Dorazio stated that under 1043(c) he had requested more time from Mr. Shustoff to talk with his client to see if she wanted to file an objection. In addition, he stated that he had not actually been retained, that he needed to meet with Ms. Tillman and find out if she wanted to be appointed, that he had not yet filed an objection, and that Ms. Tillman does not want to be Administrator or Co-Administrator. The Court informed counsel that any objections should be in writing. Mr. Dorazio stated that they only objected to the Administrator being appointed under the Independent Administration of Estates Act. Mr. Shustoff agreed to give notice to Ms. Tillman through her counsel, Mr. Dorazio, which was acceptable to Mr. Dorazio. Mr. Dorazio inquired as to the bond amount which was set by the Court at $10,000.00. The petition was granted (an order for the same was signed in open court).

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jun-03-2009 Continued To Jun-17-09 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
